text.title: Small Team Comms Rhythm Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that keep a 2-3 person team aligned without adopting scrum ceremonies or formal comms-management overhead.

rule.statement: The team MUST have exactly three rituals and no more: async daily pulse (text), weekly live sync (<=30 min), monthly direction check (<=45 min); a fourth recurring meeting is a graduation signal, not an enhancement.

rationale.source: Empirical micro-agency cadence cohort 2023-2025; team-size research

rule.rationale: A three-ritual cap is the converged minimum that prevents both drift (too little) and ceremony tax (too much) at this team size.

rule.statement: The founder MUST NOT be the only person reporting in any ritual; every participant produces their own pulse / status — even the founder is one voice among 2-3, not the moderator.

rationale.source: Equal-voice research; Edmondson psychological safety findings

rule.rationale: When the founder runs comms as broadcasts, the second person stops bringing surfacing-worthy issues and rhythm becomes status-theatre.

rule.statement: The daily pulse MUST be async (text, threaded) and time-bounded so it never replaces working hours; standups-on-Zoom are explicitly forbidden at this team size.
rule.rationale: Daily live standups cost 30-45 person-minutes/day for a 2-3 person team — overhead that does not buy anything at this size.

rule.statement: The weekly sync MUST be <=30 minutes wall-clock with a written agenda published >= 24 hours in advance; meetings without an agenda are postponed or cancelled.
rule.rationale: 30 min forces ruthless prioritisation; 24h agenda lead lets participants think before reacting.

rule.statement: When the team grows to >= 4 people, this methodology MUST be retired and the team MUST adopt a different framework (formal PM plan, scrum-lite, etc.) within the next monthly check; ad-hoc continuation past 3 is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Rhythm relies on every voice being equal and meetings <= 30 min — both break at 4+ people.
